name: openvas
_marker_lesson: The marker is the `Severity (CVSS: X.Y)` line that sits immediately ABOVE each `NVT:` line. v1 originally marked at `NVT:` itself, which left the severity header in the previous segment and forced the LLM to guess severity (often LOG) — misclassification on Ingreslock/Telnet in the bWAPP report. Breaking one line earlier makes the header travel with its NVT. One marker line == one finding.

context._header_lesson: Port/protocol headers ('High 443/tcp') appear in three layouts: plain, reversed order (markdown extractor), and with a '2.1.1 ' section-number prefix. The latest header above a marker becomes that block's port/protocol context.
context._host_lesson: The scanned IP sits on the nearest non-blank line above 'Host scan start', in the report preamble (before any marker) — context tracking is the only way to recover it.
